metadata.title:
BIG JOLT FOR
s. 8 P, co.

pages.raw_text:
One more huge pile of dollars was
added this morning, to the gigantic
price that must be paid by the Spo-
kane & Inland Empire Railway sys-
TEN for lives lost in the Occur d.
Alene wreck. on the afternoon of
July 81. In the court of Henry l..
KEENAN,, a judgement for the sum
of S11 500 was handed down to Mar
garet M. Veine of Springfield, Mo,
whose young husband was among
the victims of that catastrophe.
Together with this sum, the young
widow will be reimbursed for other
expense to which she has been
put by the litigation.


Mrs. Neine is the executriX of her
husbands WILL made in September,
1908 and by the merits of that docu-
ment, she is bequeathed the entire
estate of her husband. John A.
Veine, the wreck victim, according
to his wife's complaint was a rail
road brakeman, aged 80 years, and
was working on a road out of their
home in Springfield, Mo.
